- February 22, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, and L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (“LCAA”) (NASDAQ: LCAA), a special purpose acquisition company formed by affiliates of L Catterton, a leading global consumer-focused investment firm, today announced the completion of their previously announced business combination (“Business Combination”). rag_info.chunk_content: New York– June 24, 2024 –  Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, today announced that Lotus Tech has entered into a convertible bond agreement with an aggregate principal amount of approximately \$110 million through a private placement from its existing strategic shareholder (the “Investment”). The investment marks the shareholder’s continuous confidence in Lotus Tech’s growth strategy and outlook. The transaction is subject to customary closing conditions and the closing is expected to take place in the near future. A description of the material terms and conditions of the convertible purchase agreement and the notes is included in the Company’s current report on Form 6-K filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. The Investment underscores the shareholder’s strong belief in Lotus Technology Inc.’s vision and core value. The expected funds from the Investment will further strengthen Lotus Technology Inc.’s cash position. “Lotus Technology Inc. is grateful to its strategic shareholder as the shareholder continues to invest and demonstrate confidence in Lotus Tech’s performance and growth potential,” said Mr. Qingfeng Feng, Chief Executive Officer of Lotus Tech. “Lotus Technology Inc. looks forward to further executing its strategy, accelerating its growth, and importantly, furthering its mission to steer the industry towards a more sustainable future.”

metadata.title_summary:
title: L CATTERTON ASIA ACQUISITION CORP NOTES TO FINANCIAL STATEMENTS  DECEMBER 31, 2022 
summary: The summary outlines the terms and conditions surrounding the redemption and potential business combination of a company that has issued Class A ordinary shares. Public shareholders who redeem their shares during a designated vote will not receive funds from the Trust Account if the company fails to complete its initial business combination within the specified period. Shareholders have the option to redeem their shares for cash based on the total funds in the Trust Account, with a current value of $10.00 per share, unaffected by any deferred underwriting commissions.

The company has extended the deadline for completing its initial business combination from March 15, 2023, to June 15, 2023, with the possibility of further extending it by up to nine months. Financial contributions from the Sponsor will support these extensions, with specific amounts allocated for each month. If the business combination is not completed by the deadline, the company will liquidate and redeem public shares based on the Trust Account's funds.

The company’s management is evaluating its ability to continue as a going concern, given uncertainties about completing the business combination by the deadline. Additionally, the company has established a lock-up period for Founder Shares and outlined conditions for the exercise and redemption of warrants, which are classified as liabilities and measured at fair value. The fair value hierarchy categorizes these assets and liabilities based on the inputs used for their valuation.

Recent developments include a merger agreement with Lotus Technology Inc., which involves a two-step merger process. The agreement specifies the treatment of shares during the merger and includes provisions for the transfer of Sponsor Shares to incentivize SPAC shareholders to waive their redemption rights. Overall, the summary emphasizes the complexities of the company's financial structure, shareholder rights, and the implications of the upcoming business combination.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Working Capital Loans 
summary: The Company has the potential to obtain Working Capital Loans from the Sponsor, its affiliates, or certain officers and directors to address transaction costs associated with a Business Combination. If the Business Combination is successful, these loans will be repaid using proceeds from the Trust Account. Conversely, if the Business Combination does not take place, repayment will be sourced from outside the Trust Account, ensuring no Trust Account funds are utilized for this purpose. Up to $1,500,000 of these loans may be convertible into warrants at $1.50 each, at the lender's discretion, although the specific terms of the loans are yet to be defined. As of December 31, 2023, and 2022, the Company had not borrowed any funds under this arrangement. Additionally, the Company has secured a commitment from the Sponsor, reaffirmed on October 2, 2023, to provide up to $500,000 in funding for its working capital needs for at least one year from the date of the financial statements.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Liquidity and Capital Resources 
summary: The summary of cash flows and working capital provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance across various periods, emphasizing cash inflows and outflows, liquidity trends, and operational efficiency. The data covers two six-month periods ending June 30 for 2023 and 2022, as well as full years ending December 31 for 2022 and 2021, detailing key metrics in operating, investing, and financing activities, along with the effects of exchange rate changes.

Key findings indicate a notable increase in net cash used in operating activities, with losses escalating significantly in both the six-month and full-year comparisons. In contrast, cash outflows from investing activities showed a decrease in the latest six-month period, while the previous full year experienced a shift from positive inflows to negative outflows. Financing activities, however, provided substantial cash inflows, reflecting a strong reliance on financing to support operations.

The impact of exchange rate fluctuations was detrimental, contributing to losses in cash balances. The company's cash position revealed a decline in net cash and restricted cash in 2023 compared to the previous year, despite an increase in opening cash balances. The company has historically funded its operations through various financing means, including convertible notes and capital contributions, but now faces challenges in securing additional liquidity for ongoing operations.

To address these challenges, the company is exploring multiple strategies for securing funding, including potential external financing linked to a Business Combination and issuing new convertible notes. However, there is uncertainty regarding the success of these strategies and the company's ability to continue operations amid ongoing profitability issues and negative cash flows.

Additionally, the company has entered into various agreements related to convertible notes and exchangeable notes, which may impact future financing needs and operational strategies. The company generates revenue in RMB and anticipates ongoing challenges related to foreign currency conversion and regulatory approvals, which could further complicate its financial stability.

In summary, while the company has a strong cash position relative to previous years, it faces significant cash flow challenges, increased operating losses, and reliance on financing, necessitating strategic improvements and liquidity management to ensure future operational viability.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Cash and Asset Flows through Our Organization 
summary: LTC operates as a holding company with no direct operations, depending on its subsidiaries in China and Europe for its activities. The company's ability to pay dividends and manage debt is contingent on the dividends received from these subsidiaries, which face significant restrictions on dividend payments due to Chinese regulatory requirements. These subsidiaries can only distribute dividends from accumulated after-tax profits and must allocate funds to statutory reserves, limiting their capacity to pay cash dividends except during solvent liquidation. LTC's board of directors, governed by Cayman Islands law, has the authority to decide on dividend distributions, which are influenced by financial performance, growth prospects, and liquidity considerations. To date, LTC has not paid cash dividends, opting instead for a stock split in March 2022, and is expected to retain earnings for business development.

LTC can fund its wholly foreign-owned subsidiaries in China through loans or capital contributions, adhering to local regulations that require governmental approval. However, the subsidiaries face strict controls on transferring funds and paying dividends, with scrutiny from banks and government oversight affecting currency conversion and cash accessibility. These regulatory challenges pose risks to LTC's liquidity and operational capabilities.

In terms of financial activities, LTC reported significant loans and capital contributions to its subsidiaries over the past three years, with a notable increase in 2023. The WFOE, a key subsidiary, engaged in substantial lending and capital contributions during this period, while also repaying loans. The financial interactions among LTC, its subsidiaries, and the former VIE reflect a complex web of funding and repayment activities, underscoring the intricacies of managing finances within the constraints of Chinese law. Overall, LTC's operational and financial strategies are heavily influenced by regulatory environments, particularly in China, which could impact its future growth and liquidity.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Working Capital Loans 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Working Capital Loans**

In the context of financing transaction costs associated with a Business Combination, the Sponsor, along with its affiliates or certain officers and directors of the Company, may provide Working Capital Loans. However, they are not obligated to do so. If a Business Combination is successfully completed, these loans will be repaid using proceeds from the Trust Account. Conversely, if the Business Combination does not close, repayment will be made from funds outside the Trust Account. In such a scenario, the Company may utilize its working capital to fulfill loan obligations, but no proceeds from the Trust Account will be utilized for this purpose.

The Working Capital Loans may allow for the conversion of up to $1,500,000 into warrants at a price of $1.50 each, which is in line with the terms of the Private Placement Warrants. It is important to note that the specific terms of these Working Capital Loans are currently undetermined, and there are no written agreements in place as of now. As of December 31, 2023, and December 31, 2022, the Company reported no borrowings under these loans.

Additionally, on April 11, 2022, and re-issued on October 2, 2023, the Company secured a commitment from the Sponsor to provide loans totaling up to $500,000 to cover working capital needs for a minimum duration of one year from the date of the financial statement issuance. This commitment underscores the Sponsor's support in ensuring the Company's operational liquidity during this period. 

In summary, while the Working Capital Loans present a potential avenue for financing, their execution and terms remain contingent on the success of the Business Combination and the ongoing commitment from the Sponsor.

title: Lotus Tech and  L  Catterton Asia Acquisition
Corp Complete Business Combination 
summary: On February 22, 2024, Lotus Technology Inc., a leading luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, announced the successful completion of its merger with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (LCAA), a special purpose acquisition company backed by L Catterton. Following shareholder approval on February 2, 2024, LCAA has become a wholly owned subsidiary of Lotus Tech, which will continue to operate under its name. The company's American depositary shares (ADSs) are set to begin trading on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol "LOT" on February 23, 2024. CEO Qingfeng Feng highlighted this listing as a significant milestone in Lotus Tech's ambition to lead in sustainable luxury mobility, while LCAA Co-CEO Chinta Bhagat expressed optimism about the growth opportunities this merger presents. To commemorate the occasion, Lotus Tech will ring the Nasdaq opening bell on February 23, 2024, with the event available for live streaming.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. is a foreign company that files a FORM 6-K with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to provide updates on significant corporate events and financial performance. This report is essential for ensuring that U.S. investors have access to timely information about the company's operations, particularly as it pertains to its financial health and corporate governance.

For the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology reported substantial growth in key financial metrics compared to the same period in 2023. Deliveries increased by 239%, reaching 4,873 units, while revenues surged to $398 million, a 206% rise. Despite these gains, the company faced rising costs, leading to an operating loss of $438 million and a net loss of $460 million for the first half of 2024. The adjusted net loss also increased, reflecting ongoing financial challenges.

The company's balance sheet as of June 30, 2024, showed total assets rising significantly to $2.5 billion, driven by increases in both current and non-current assets. However, total liabilities also grew, resulting in a shareholders' deficit that improved but remained negative. Notably, the company eliminated mezzanine equity, indicating a shift in its capital structure.

Lotus Technology's product lineup includes several high-performance vehicle models, with projections indicating continued growth and profitability by 2026. The company anticipates producing over 30,000 units and achieving a gross profit margin exceeding 20%. Recent product launches, including limited-edition models, highlight the brand's focus on luxury and exclusivity.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates significant revenue growth and operational advancements, it continues to navigate challenges related to rising costs and net losses. The financial data presented underscores both the company's potential and the need for careful management of its financial resources as it aims for future profitability.

This report is essential for ensuring that U.S. investors have access to timely information about the company's operations, particularly as it pertains to its financial health and corporate governance.

For the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology reported substantial growth in key financial metrics compared to the same period in 2023. Deliveries increased by 239%, reaching 4,873 units, while revenues surged to $398 million, a 206% rise. Despite these gains, the company faced rising costs, leading to an operating loss of $438 million and a net loss of $460 million for the first half of 2024. The adjusted net loss also increased, reflecting ongoing financial challenges.

The company's balance sheet as of June 30, 2024, showed total assets rising significantly to $2.5 billion, driven by increases in both current and non-current assets. However, total liabilities also grew, resulting in a shareholders' deficit that improved but remained negative. Notably, the company eliminated mezzanine equity, indicating a shift in its capital structure.

Lotus Technology's product lineup includes several high-performance vehicle models, with projections indicating continued growth and profitability by 2026. The company anticipates producing over 30,000 units and achieving a gross profit margin exceeding 20%. Recent product launches, including limited-edition models, highlight the brand's focus on luxury and exclusivity.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates significant revenue growth and operational advancements, it continues to navigate challenges related to rising costs and net losses. The financial data presented underscores both the company's potential and the need for careful management of its financial resources as it aims for future profitability.

- **Row 23:** The combined total of liabilities, mezzanine equity, and shareholders’ deficit increased to **$2,506,254** as of June 30, 2024, from **$1,584,679** as of December 31, 2023.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: In February 1983, Team Lotus announced the installation at the team's Ketteringham Hall base of a massive oven capable of swallowing an entire racing car chassis, and wheeled out the fruits of its first new 'recipe', the John Player Special Type 93T. Still reeling from the sudden, unexpected death of Colin Chapman, the team felt it had to press on, and had reasonable cause for optimism with its all carbon-fibre/Kevlar chassis and what was considered the best engine in Formula 1 to power it. The new car promised to be "even stronger, lighter yet stiffer." It was also built larger to afford the driver greater protection, using space-age materials including a carbon-fibre/Kevlar mix which was pre-soaked in resin (a world-first) and built into layer form with a Nomex paper honeycomb forming the meat in the sandwich. The entire ensemble was then, in the words of the factory press handout, "literally baked at 125C." The team also announced a switch to radial-ply Pirellis, to Elf lubricants, and to Champion spark plugs, but of course, the most important new ingredient was that designated by the suffix 'T' – Renault's V6, 90-degree 1,492cc EF1 engine, with twin KKK turbochargers.

summary: The M&A Rules in China, established by six regulatory authorities in 2006 and revised in 2009, require offshore special purpose vehicles controlled by Chinese entities to obtain approval from the China Securities Regulatory Commission (CSRC) before listing on foreign stock exchanges. The interpretation of these regulations is often unclear, leading to concerns about the necessity and timeline for obtaining CSRC approval, which, if delayed or revoked, could result in significant penalties such as fines and restrictions on operations. The Chinese government is increasing its scrutiny of overseas offerings and foreign investments, emphasizing cross-border regulatory cooperation and the establishment of a comprehensive framework for applying PRC capital market laws internationally.

On February 17, 2023, the CSRC introduced the Overseas Listing Filing Rules, effective March 31, 2023, which outline the filing requirements for PRC companies seeking to list abroad. Companies must file with the CSRC within three business days of submitting IPO application documents or making a public announcement. The rules also specify that companies must secure any necessary prior approvals from other regulatory authorities before filing with the CSRC. Furthermore, listed companies must report significant changes in their business or control within three business days and comply with additional filing requirements for follow-on offerings.

Recent cybersecurity regulations require online platform operators with over one million users to undergo a cybersecurity review before foreign listings, adding another layer of compliance complexity. The PRC Cybersecurity Law mandates stringent security measures for data protection, with entities classified by risk levels needing to report to authorities. Uncertainties surrounding the interpretation of these laws could impact companies seeking to list overseas.

Lotus Tech, which has filed with the CSRC regarding its Business Combination, believes that the Overseas Listing Filing Rules do not apply to its offering. However, the company acknowledges the potential risks of non-compliance with regulatory requirements, which could lead to significant costs, penalties, and reputational damage, ultimately affecting its ability to attract foreign investments and maintain its listing on international exchanges.

summary: The M&A Rules, established by Chinese regulatory authorities in 2006 and amended in 2009, require offshore special purpose vehicles controlled by PRC companies to obtain approval from the China Securities Regulatory Commission (CSRC) before listing on foreign exchanges. These regulations, aimed at overseeing acquisitions and overseas listings, have ambiguous enforcement, raising concerns about approval timelines and the potential for revocation, which could lead to fines and operational restrictions. 

In response to increasing scrutiny over overseas offerings, the CSRC introduced the Overseas Listing Filing Rules on February 17, 2023, mandating that PRC companies file with the CSRC before conducting any overseas offerings. Companies must complete filing procedures swiftly and report significant changes post-filing. Non-compliance can result in penalties for both the issuer and its shareholders. 

Additionally, the Revised Measures for Cybersecurity Review require online platform operators with substantial user data to undergo a cybersecurity review prior to foreign listings, adding another layer of regulatory complexity. The PRC Cybersecurity Law further mandates stringent security measures for data protection, with a grading system determining compliance obligations.

Recent regulations, including the Archives Rules effective March 31, 2023, impose confidentiality and archiving requirements on domestic companies engaging in overseas offerings, necessitating approvals for disclosing sensitive materials. 

Lotus Tech, in particular, faces uncertainties regarding its filing with the CSRC for its Business Combination before listing on Nasdaq. Delays or failures in obtaining necessary approvals could lead to significant operational and financial repercussions, including sanctions from regulatory bodies. The company also risks restrictions on foreign investments, which could adversely affect its market performance and the value of its securities. Overall, the evolving regulatory landscape presents substantial risks for Lotus Tech and similar companies navigating overseas listings.

title: Mainland China 
summary: The PRC Enterprise Income Tax Law, effective since January 1, 2008, and last amended on December 29, 2018, imposes a standard income tax rate of 25% on subsidiaries and Variable Interest Entities (VIEs) in mainland China, unless otherwise specified. Companies are also subject to Value Added Tax (VAT) at rates of 13% and 6% for products and services, with additional surcharges as per PRC regulations. Dividends from wholly foreign-owned subsidiaries in China to Hong Kong holding companies face a typical 10% withholding tax, which can be reduced to 5% if the Hong Kong entity meets certain criteria under the tax avoidance arrangement and submits an application, although prior approval is no longer necessary. If a company based in the Cayman Islands or its subsidiaries are classified as "resident enterprises," they would incur a 25% enterprise income tax on global income, potentially leading to adverse tax implications for the company and its non-PRC shareholders. Additionally, the law allows for super deductions on R&D expenses, with enhanced benefits for intangible assets recognized after January 1, 2023, allowing deductions of 100% and amortization at 200% of their cost, significantly increasing the tax incentives for R&D activities.

summary: The summary of cash flows and working capital provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance across various periods, emphasizing cash inflows and outflows, liquidity trends, and operational efficiency. The data covers two six-month periods ending June 30 for 2023 and 2022, as well as full years ending December 31 for 2022 and 2021, detailing key metrics in operating, investing, and financing activities, along with the effects of exchange rate changes.

Key findings indicate a notable increase in net cash used in operating activities, with losses escalating significantly in both the six-month and full-year comparisons. In contrast, cash outflows from investing activities showed a decrease in the latest six-month period, while the previous full year experienced a shift from positive inflows to negative outflows. Financing activities, however, provided substantial cash inflows, reflecting a strong reliance on financing to support operations.

The impact of exchange rate fluctuations was detrimental, contributing to losses in cash balances. The company's cash position revealed a decline in net cash and restricted cash in 2023 compared to the previous year, despite an increase in opening cash balances. The company has historically funded its operations through various financing means, including convertible notes and capital contributions, but now faces challenges in securing additional liquidity for ongoing operations.

To address these challenges, the company is exploring multiple strategies for securing funding, including potential external financing linked to a Business Combination and issuing new convertible notes. However, there is uncertainty regarding the success of these strategies and the company's ability to continue operations amid ongoing profitability issues and negative cash flows.

Additionally, the company has entered into various agreements related to convertible notes and exchangeable notes, which may impact future financing needs and operational strategies. The company generates revenue in RMB and anticipates ongoing challenges related to foreign currency conversion and regulatory approvals, which could further complicate its financial stability.

In summary, while the company has a strong cash position relative to previous years, it faces significant cash flow challenges, increased operating losses, and reliance on financing, necessitating strategic improvements and liquidity management to ensure future operational viability.

title: Material cash requirements 
summary: As of December 31, 2023, the company faces significant cash requirements beyond regular operational expenses, primarily for capital expenditures, purchase commitments, and operating lease obligations. In 2023, capital expenditures reached $213.7 million, a notable increase from $133.3 million in 2022 and $34.6 million in 2021, with a planned commitment of $59.6 million to support ongoing business operations. The total purchase commitment stands at $182.0 million, mainly for future minimum purchases of BEV lifestyle models, Lotus-brand sports cars, and R&D services. Additionally, operating lease obligations amount to $152.6 million, covering various non-cancellable leases for offices, warehouses, and equipment globally.

The company also has outstanding financial liabilities, including $101.9 million in convertible notes, $454.3 million in exchangeable notes, and $11.9 million in put option liabilities. To address its current and future cash needs, the company intends to utilize existing cash reserves and explore various financing options.

A summary of the company’s financial commitments reveals total obligations of $962.3 million, with $661.7 million due within one year and $300.6 million due beyond one year. Key commitments include capital expenditures of $59.6 million, purchase commitments of $182.0 million, operating lease obligations of $152.6 million, convertible notes totaling $101.9 million, exchangeable notes of $454.3 million, and put option liabilities of $11.9 million. The company confirms that there are no additional significant commitments or off-balance sheet arrangements beyond those outlined.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 30. SUBSEQUENT EVENTS 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Subsequent Events**

The chapter on Subsequent Events outlines several significant developments impacting the Company following the reporting period ending December 31, 2023. 

1. **Merger Transaction:** A key event was the completion of a merger transaction on February 22, 2024, as detailed in note 1(b). This strategic move is expected to enhance the Company's market position and operational capabilities.

2. **PIPE Investments:** On February 15, 2024, the Group entered into a new PIPE (Private Investment in Public Equity) investment agreement with an investor, as noted in note 13. This investment is anticipated to provide additional capital to support the Company's growth initiatives.

3. **Share Options Granted:** Between January 1, 2024, and March 31, 2024, the Company granted a total of 5,972,760 share options to employees under the 2022 Share Incentive Plan. The options have a weighted average exercise price of US$2.89 per option, aligning employee interests with the Company's performance.

4. **Loans to Related Party:** On March 11, 2024, the Company’s subsidiary entered into a loan agreement with a related party controlled by the Controlling Shareholder. This agreement involves providing unsecured loans totaling GBP 275,000 (approximately US$354,000) for a term of 5 years at an annual interest rate of 6.95%. This transaction raises considerations regarding related party transactions and potential conflicts of interest.

5. **Termination of Pledge:** In April 2024, the Company terminated the pledge related to the land use right valued at US$32,653 and corporate buildings under construction valued at US$37,287. These assets were previously included in the operating lease right-of-use assets and property, equipment, and software as of December 31, 2023. The termination of this pledge may affect the Company’s asset management and financial leverage.

This summary encapsulates the critical subsequent events that may influence the Company's financial position and operational strategy moving forward.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Related Party Transactions 
summary: ### Comprehensive Summary of Related Party Transactions

The "Related Party Transactions" chapter outlines the company's extensive financial interactions with related parties over the years, particularly focusing on the sale of sports cars, BEV lifestyle models, auto parts, and consulting services. 

#### Revenue from Related Party Transactions
- **Service Revenue**: The company generated revenue from services provided to related parties amounting to:
  - **2023**: US$12.6 million
  - **2022**: US$8.3 million
  - **2021**: US$3.3 million
- **Product Sales**: Sales of products to related parties were:
  - **2023**: US$3.4 million
  - **2022**: US$23 thousand
  - **2021**: None

#### Accounts Receivable and Payments
- As of December 31, 2023, accounts receivable from related parties totaled **US$22.4 million**, up from **US$8.5 million** in 2022.
- The company made payments on behalf of related parties totaling:
  - **2023**: US$4.0 million
  - **2022**: US$244 thousand
  - **2021**: None
- Related parties collected **US$3.7 million** in 2023.

#### Loans and Prepayments
- Unsecured short-term loans to related parties were:
  - **2023**: US$1.2 million
  - **2022**: US$2.3 million
- Interest income from these loans was recognized at:
  - **2023**: US$122 thousand
  - **2022**: US$24 thousand
- Prepayments for vehicle purchases to related parties amounted to:
  - **2023**: US$21.4 million
  - **2022**: US$6.1 million
- An expected credit loss provision for these prepayments increased from **US$12 thousand** on January 1, 2023, to **US$23 thousand** by December 31, 2023.

#### Purchases from Related Parties
- Total purchases from related parties were significant, amounting to:
  - **2023**: US$852.6 million (with **US$549.9 million** recognized as cost of goods sold)
  - **2022**: US$44.3 million
  - **2021**: US$14.3 million
- Research and development expenses related to these purchases were:
  - **2023**: US$134.7 million
  - **2022**: US$167.0 million
  - **2021**: US$47.4 million
- Equipment and software purchases from related parties totaled **US$11.2 million** in 2023 and **US$42.8 million** in 2022.
- The company incurred short-term lease costs of **US$765 thousand** in 2023.

#### Agreements and Investments
- In 2023, the company established a distribution agreement with Lotus Cars Limited, recording a payable of **US$23.0 million** for distribution rights.
- The company had previously entered into a license agreement for the Geely License, expensing **US$288.9 million** as R&D costs in 2021.
- Additionally, equity interests were acquired in related entities, including a **US$15.5 million** transaction with Geely UK Limited completed in June 2022.
- The fair value of a convertible note purchased from ECARX Holding Inc. was **US$3.3 million** as of December 31, 2023.

Overall, the transactions with related parties reflect significant financial interactions that have notably impacted both revenues and expenses across multiple years, underscoring the importance of these relationships in the company's financial landscape.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Working Capital Loans 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of Working Capital Loans**

In the context of financing transaction costs associated with a Business Combination, the Sponsor, along with its affiliates or certain officers and directors of the Company, may provide Working Capital Loans. However, they are not obligated to do so. If a Business Combination is successfully completed, these loans will be repaid using proceeds from the Trust Account. Conversely, if the Business Combination does not close, repayment will be made from funds outside the Trust Account. In such a scenario, the Company may utilize its working capital to fulfill loan obligations, but no proceeds from the Trust Account will be utilized for this purpose.

The Working Capital Loans may allow for the conversion of up to $1,500,000 into warrants at a price of $1.50 each, which is in line with the terms of the Private Placement Warrants. It is important to note that the specific terms of these Working Capital Loans are currently undetermined, and there are no written agreements in place as of now. As of December 31, 2023, and December 31, 2022, the Company reported no borrowings under these loans.

Additionally, on April 11, 2022, and re-issued on October 2, 2023, the Company secured a commitment from the Sponsor to provide loans totaling up to $500,000 to cover working capital needs for a minimum duration of one year from the date of the financial statement issuance. This commitment underscores the Sponsor's support in ensuring the Company's operational liquidity during this period. 

In summary, while the Working Capital Loans present a potential avenue for financing, their execution and terms remain contingent on the success of the Business Combination and the ongoing commitment from the Sponsor.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology’s American Depositary Shares will begin trading on the Nasdaq on February 23, 2024, under the ticker symbol “LOT.” New York and Singapore - February 20, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, and L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (“L CAA”) (NASDAQ: LCAA), a special purpose acquisition company formed by affiliates of L Catterton, a leading global consumer-focused investment firm, announced today that their previously proposed business combination is expected to be completed on February 22, 2024. The combined company will retain Lotus Technology Inc.'s name as “Lotus Technology Inc.” and its American Depositary Shares (ADS) will commence trading on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol “LOT” on February 23, 2024. The business combination was approved by L CAA shareholders at an extraordinary general meeting held on February 2, 2024. Integrating Lotus Technology’s sports car DNA with a new generation of premium lifestyle vehicles, Lotus Technology Inc. is committed to transforming the brand into an advanced, fully electric, intelligent, and sustainable luxury mobility provider before its 80th anniversary in 2028. Lotus Technology Inc. has already set this ambitious plan in motion by launching a leading product portfolio of high-performing battery electric vehicles (“BEVs”) ahead of other global luxury automakers. These BEVs include Eletre, a new breed of all-electric hyper-SUV powered by Lotus Technology’s proprietary 800-volt EPA, and Emeya, an all-electric hyper-GT among the most advanced vehicles of its kind in the world.

Corp Announce Closing of Business Combination 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc., a luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, is poised to make its debut on the Nasdaq stock exchange on February 23, 2024, trading under the ticker symbol "LOT." This significant milestone follows the completion of its merger with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (LCAA), which is set to finalize on February 22, 2024, after receiving shareholder approval on February 2, 2024. Lotus Tech aims to transform into a fully electric and sustainable luxury mobility provider by 2028, coinciding with its 80th anniversary, and plans to achieve a 100% electric product portfolio by 2027. The company has already introduced high-performing battery electric vehicles, such as the Eletre hyper-SUV and the Emeya hyper-GT, with plans for two additional models in the next two years. Since announcing the merger, Lotus Tech has secured over $880 million in financing commitments, marking one of the largest de-SPAC transactions since 2023. CEO Qingfeng Feng expressed enthusiasm for the company's growth as a public entity, while L CAA Co-CEO Chinta Bhagat highlighted their shared vision for establishing a leading luxury electric vehicle brand. To commemorate its public listing, Lotus Tech will ring the Nasdaq opening bell on February 23, an event that will be live-streamed for viewers.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. is a foreign company that files a FORM 6-K with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to provide updates on significant corporate events and financial performance. This report is essential for ensuring that U.S. investors have access to timely information about the company's operations, particularly as it pertains to its financial health and corporate governance.

For the first half of 2024, Lotus Technology reported substantial growth in key financial metrics compared to the same period in 2023. Deliveries increased by 239%, reaching 4,873 units, while revenues surged to $398 million, a 206% rise. Despite these gains, the company faced rising costs, leading to an operating loss of $438 million and a net loss of $460 million for the first half of 2024. The adjusted net loss also increased, reflecting ongoing financial challenges.

The company's balance sheet as of June 30, 2024, showed total assets rising significantly to $2.5 billion, driven by increases in both current and non-current assets. However, total liabilities also grew, resulting in a shareholders' deficit that improved but remained negative. Notably, the company eliminated mezzanine equity, indicating a shift in its capital structure.

Lotus Technology's product lineup includes several high-performance vehicle models, with projections indicating continued growth and profitability by 2026. The company anticipates producing over 30,000 units and achieving a gross profit margin exceeding 20%. Recent product launches, including limited-edition models, highlight the brand's focus on luxury and exclusivity.

Overall, while Lotus Technology Inc. demonstrates significant revenue growth and operational advancements, it continues to navigate challenges related to rising costs and net losses. The financial data presented underscores both the company's potential and the need for careful management of its financial resources as it aims for future profitability.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: New York and Singapore - February 23, 2024 – Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”), a leading global luxury electric vehicle maker, rang today’s opening bell at the Nasdaq stock exchange in New York City in celebration of its public listing. The Company’s American depositary shares (“ADSs”) commenced trading today on the Nasdaq Stock Market LLC (“Nasdaq”) under the ticker symbol “LOT” following the completion of its business combination on February 22, 2024, with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (“L CAA”), a special purpose acquisition company (“SPAC”) formed by affiliates of L Catterton, a leading global consumer-focused investment firm. Lotus Technology Inc. CEO Feng Qingfeng was joined by members of the Lotus Technology Inc. and L Catterton teams in New York City to witness this important milestone, which will help Lotus Technology Inc. to further its mission of becoming an advanced, fully electric, intelligent, and sustainable luxury mobility provider under the brand’s Vision80 strategy. “This is an exciting moment for all of us at Lotus Technology Inc.,” said Mr. Feng. “I would like to extend my appreciation to our shareholders, partners, suppliers, our outstanding employees, and most importantly, our customers, for their support.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology Celebrates Public Listing
on Nasdaq 
summary: Lotus Technology Inc. (Lotus Tech), a leading luxury electric vehicle manufacturer, marked a significant milestone by going public on February 23, 2024, with its shares trading on the Nasdaq under the ticker "LOT." This event followed its merger with L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp (LCAA), a SPAC linked to the global investment firm L Catterton. The public listing is a crucial step in Lotus Tech's ambition to become a leader in advanced, fully electric, and sustainable luxury mobility, aligning with its Vision80 strategy. CEO Feng Qingfeng celebrated the achievement alongside the L Catterton team, expressing gratitude to stakeholders and emphasizing the company's commitment to its heritage and future growth.

Chinta Bhagat, Co-CEO of LCAA, highlighted the potential for Lotus Tech to thrive in the expanding luxury electric vehicle market, aided by insights from their partnership with LVMH. The listing is expected to accelerate Lotus Tech's development of next-generation automotive technologies and expand its global distribution network from around 200 to over 300 stores by 2025. The company aims to be the first traditional luxury auto brand to offer a fully electric lineup by 2027, with plans to introduce additional high-performance models, including the Eletre hyper-SUV and Emeya hyper-GT, in the coming years. The celebratory bell-ringing ceremony was broadcasted from Times Square, featuring Lotus's signature battery electric vehicle models, allowing global employees to join in the festivities.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology Inc. is proud to carry Lotus’s heritage into the future and to open a new chapter in the Lotus story with our public listing today.” Chinta Bhagat, Co-Chief Executive Officer of L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp, said: “L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp believes that Lotus Technology Inc. is ideally positioned to seize the tremendous opportunity in the fast-growing and underserved luxury electric vehicle market, leveraging our global consumer insights and strategic relationship with LVMH. Today’s public listing is a significant step in Lotus Technology Inc.’s future expansion, and L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp is looking forward to continued collaboration with Lotus Technology Inc.’s talented management team.” The Nasdaq listing will support Lotus Technology Inc.’s development of next generation automobility technologies and the expected expansion of its global distribution network from around 200 to over 300 stores globally by 2025. Set to become the first traditional luxury auto brand to achieve a 100% electric product portfolio by 2027, Lotus Technology Inc. has launched a well-balanced portfolio of exceptionally performant models, including the Eletre hyper-SUV and Emeya hyper-GT, and plans to launch two additional luxury battery electric vehicles (BEV) over the next two years. The bell-ringing ceremony was broadcast on Nasdaq’s digital billboards in Times Square and livestreamed online, with Lotus Technology Inc. employees joining the celebration remotely from offices across the world. The brand’s signature battery electric vehicle models, Evija, Eletre, and Emeya, were also displayed in Times Square in front of the Nasdaq stock exchange.

summary: The summary outlines key definitions and terms related to American Depositary Shares (ADS), Ordinary Shares, and various corporate governance aspects of a company. An "ADS" represents Ordinary Shares, while "Affiliate" refers to individuals or entities with controlling relationships, including family members and entities they own. The "Board of Directors" oversees the company's management, and "Business Day" excludes weekends and certain holidays in specified regions.

"Capital Stock" encompasses all forms of equity ownership, and "Common Equity" typically grants voting rights in corporate governance. "Control" involves owning more than 50% of voting power, while "Conversion Date," "Conversion Notice," and "Conversion Period" pertain to the specifics of convertible notes. The "Current Market Price" for an ADS is calculated based on the average sale prices over a defined period, with adjustments made for dividends.

The document also defines "Event of Default," "Fundamental Change," and the implications of significant ownership changes, including conditions that trigger a Fundamental Change such as mergers or asset transfers. A "change in law" may impact the company's operations and economic benefits, and specific conditions are outlined for transactions that do not qualify as a Fundamental Change.

Additional terms include "Governmental Authority," "Holder," and "Ordinary Share," along with definitions for various corporate roles and documents, such as "Officer's Certificate" and "Significant Subsidiary." The "Purchase Agreement" and "Subsidiary" are also defined, while "Trading Day" specifies when trading occurs for ADSs or related securities. Overall, the summary provides a comprehensive overview of the legal and operational framework governing the company's equity and related transactions.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: In February 1983, Team Lotus announced the installation at the team's Ketteringham Hall base of a massive oven capable of swallowing an entire racing car chassis, and wheeled out the fruits of its first new 'recipe', the John Player Special Type 93T. Still reeling from the sudden, unexpected death of Colin Chapman, the team felt it had to press on, and had reasonable cause for optimism with its all carbon-fibre/Kevlar chassis and what was considered the best engine in Formula 1 to power it. The new car promised to be "even stronger, lighter yet stiffer." It was also built larger to afford the driver greater protection, using space-age materials including a carbon-fibre/Kevlar mix which was pre-soaked in resin (a world-first) and built into layer form with a Nomex paper honeycomb forming the meat in the sandwich. The entire ensemble was then, in the words of the factory press handout, "literally baked at 125C." The team also announced a switch to radial-ply Pirellis, to Elf lubricants, and to Champion spark plugs, but of course, the most important new ingredient was that designated by the suffix 'T' – Renault's V6, 90-degree 1,492cc EF1 engine, with twin KKK turbochargers.

summary: The M&A Rules in China, established by six regulatory authorities in 2006 and revised in 2009, require offshore special purpose vehicles controlled by Chinese entities to obtain approval from the China Securities Regulatory Commission (CSRC) before listing on foreign stock exchanges. The interpretation of these regulations is often unclear, leading to concerns about the necessity and timeline for obtaining CSRC approval, which, if delayed or revoked, could result in significant penalties such as fines and restrictions on operations. The Chinese government is increasing its scrutiny of overseas offerings and foreign investments, emphasizing cross-border regulatory cooperation and the establishment of a comprehensive framework for applying PRC capital market laws internationally.

On February 17, 2023, the CSRC introduced the Overseas Listing Filing Rules, effective March 31, 2023, which outline the filing requirements for PRC companies seeking to list abroad. Companies must file with the CSRC within three business days of submitting IPO application documents or making a public announcement. The rules also specify that companies must secure any necessary prior approvals from other regulatory authorities before filing with the CSRC. Furthermore, listed companies must report significant changes in their business or control within three business days and comply with additional filing requirements for follow-on offerings.

Recent cybersecurity regulations require online platform operators with over one million users to undergo a cybersecurity review before foreign listings, adding another layer of compliance complexity. The PRC Cybersecurity Law mandates stringent security measures for data protection, with entities classified by risk levels needing to report to authorities. Uncertainties surrounding the interpretation of these laws could impact companies seeking to list overseas.

Lotus Tech, which has filed with the CSRC regarding its Business Combination, believes that the Overseas Listing Filing Rules do not apply to its offering. However, the company acknowledges the potential risks of non-compliance with regulatory requirements, which could lead to significant costs, penalties, and reputational damage, ultimately affecting its ability to attract foreign investments and maintain its listing on international exchanges.

summary: The M&A Rules, established by Chinese regulatory authorities in 2006 and amended in 2009, require offshore special purpose vehicles controlled by PRC companies to obtain approval from the China Securities Regulatory Commission (CSRC) before listing on foreign exchanges. These regulations, aimed at overseeing acquisitions and overseas listings, have ambiguous enforcement, raising concerns about approval timelines and the potential for revocation, which could lead to fines and operational restrictions. 

In response to increasing scrutiny over overseas offerings, the CSRC introduced the Overseas Listing Filing Rules on February 17, 2023, mandating that PRC companies file with the CSRC before conducting any overseas offerings. Companies must complete filing procedures swiftly and report significant changes post-filing. Non-compliance can result in penalties for both the issuer and its shareholders. 

Additionally, the Revised Measures for Cybersecurity Review require online platform operators with substantial user data to undergo a cybersecurity review prior to foreign listings, adding another layer of regulatory complexity. The PRC Cybersecurity Law further mandates stringent security measures for data protection, with a grading system determining compliance obligations.

Recent regulations, including the Archives Rules effective March 31, 2023, impose confidentiality and archiving requirements on domestic companies engaging in overseas offerings, necessitating approvals for disclosing sensitive materials. 

Lotus Tech, in particular, faces uncertainties regarding its filing with the CSRC for its Business Combination before listing on Nasdaq. Delays or failures in obtaining necessary approvals could lead to significant operational and financial repercussions, including sanctions from regulatory bodies. The company also risks restrictions on foreign investments, which could adversely affect its market performance and the value of its securities. Overall, the evolving regulatory landscape presents substantial risks for Lotus Tech and similar companies navigating overseas listings.

title: Mainland China 
summary: The PRC Enterprise Income Tax Law, effective since January 1, 2008, and last amended on December 29, 2018, imposes a standard income tax rate of 25% on subsidiaries and Variable Interest Entities (VIEs) in mainland China, unless otherwise specified. Companies are also subject to Value Added Tax (VAT) at rates of 13% and 6% for products and services, with additional surcharges as per PRC regulations. Dividends from wholly foreign-owned subsidiaries in China to Hong Kong holding companies face a typical 10% withholding tax, which can be reduced to 5% if the Hong Kong entity meets certain criteria under the tax avoidance arrangement and submits an application, although prior approval is no longer necessary. If a company based in the Cayman Islands or its subsidiaries are classified as "resident enterprises," they would incur a 25% enterprise income tax on global income, potentially leading to adverse tax implications for the company and its non-PRC shareholders. Additionally, the law allows for super deductions on R&D expenses, with enhanced benefits for intangible assets recognized after January 1, 2023, allowing deductions of 100% and amortization at 200% of their cost, significantly increasing the tax incentives for R&D activities.
